evals.name: greenfield-high-confidence-capture
evals.prompt: From now on we always use Vitest, not Jest. We migrated last quarter and the Jest config is only there for one legacy package.
evals.expected_output: Creates docs/learnings/ and docs/learnings/INDEX.md (neither existed). Writes docs/learnings/convention-use-vitest.md with type: convention, a Why that references the migration, and a How to apply that names the legacy exception. Appends a line to CLAUDE.md under '## Learnings' because the user used 'always' + 'from now on'. Final report names the file, confirms index + CLAUDE.md updated.

evals.name: dedupe-on-capture
evals.prompt: Hey, for next time remember we use Vitest not Jest in this repo — reviewer flagged a PR that still had Jest imports.
evals.expected_output: Reads docs/learnings/INDEX.md, finds existing convention-use-vitest.md, runs update mode (not capture). Merges the new source (MR review comment) into the existing file. Does NOT create a duplicate convention-vitest-* file. Report says UPDATED, not CAPTURED.

evals.name: low-confidence-no-claude-md
evals.prompt: Can you note that our auth tests tend to use the seedUserWithRole fixture? Not a hard rule, just something I've noticed.
evals.expected_output: Captures as a pattern (pattern-auth-test-seeding.md or similar). Writes the learning file and updates INDEX.md. Does NOT update CLAUDE.md because the user said 'tend to' and 'not a hard rule' — no absolute language, so this stays in docs/learnings/ only. Report confirms CLAUDE.md was NOT updated and explains why.

evals.name: remove-learning
evals.prompt: /learn remove convention-use-vitest — we're moving back to Jest for the new service, this rule is stale.
evals.expected_output: Confirms the slug exists in INDEX.md. Deletes docs/learnings/convention-use-vitest.md. Removes the pointer line from INDEX.md. Removes the matching bullet from CLAUDE.md's ## Learnings section (leaving the section header intact even if empty). Report lists exactly what was removed from where.

evals.name: contradiction-detected
evals.prompt: From now on we use Jest, not Vitest. New service is Jest-only.
evals.expected_output: Reads INDEX.md and finds the existing convention-use-vitest.md. Detects the new rule contradicts it (Vitest vs Jest) rather than refining it. Stops and asks the user to resolve the conflict before writing anything. Does NOT silently overwrite, does NOT create a second contradictory file, does NOT update CLAUDE.md. Report makes the conflict explicit and names both the existing learning and the new observation.

evals.name: skill-quality-capture
evals.prompt: prd-refine keeps missing migration sub-tasks when we add new models. Happened again on APP-201 — reviewer had to send it back. We should always include a migration sub-task when the plan touches a model file.
evals.expected_output: Captures as type: skill-quality (skill-prd-refine-missing-migrations.md or similar). Frontmatter includes skill: prd-refine. Body names the recurring failure mode, the Why (CI requires migration and code in same MR), and the How to apply (verify the Implementation Plan includes a migration sub-task with db/migrate/*.rb paths when a model changes). The user said 'always' so CLAUDE.md gets updated under ## Learnings. Report confirms the skill name was recorded.
